title: Tables
summary: Tables show data in rows and columns.
The table element wraps the whole thing.
Rows go in tr, header cells in th, and normal cells in td.
Tables are for data, not for page layout.

sections.text: When information naturally fits a grid, like a schedule or a price list, a table is the right tool. HTML builds tables out of a few simple pieces.

sections.title: The Building Blocks
sections.text: A table starts with the table element. Inside it, each row is a tr. Inside each row, a normal cell is a td, and a heading cell, like a column title, is a th. Stack rows and cells together and you get a grid.

sections.title: Tables Are for Data
sections.text: Tables are meant for real data that belongs in rows and columns, like a timetable. They are not for arranging the layout of a page. Long ago people misused them for layout, but CSS does that job far better now.

media.alt: A simple table with a header row and several data rows

sections.title: A Spreadsheet
sections.text: A table is just like a spreadsheet. The table is the whole sheet, each tr is a row, and each td is a single cell. The th cells are the bold labels across the top that tell you what each column means.

media.alt: A spreadsheet grid with labelled columns and filled cells

sections.title: Building a Simple Table
items: Add a tr for each row you need.
In the top row, use th cells for the column titles.
In the other rows, use td cells for the data.
The browser lines them up into a neat grid.

sections.text: Adding a caption element inside a table gives it a title, which helps screen reader users understand what the table is about before they read it.

terms.definition: The element that wraps a whole table.
A single row in a table.
A heading cell, like a column title.
A title for the table that aids understanding.

options: Showing data in rows and columns
questions.explanation: Tables are for showing data that belongs in rows and columns.

questions.text: What element is a single row?
questions.explanation: Each row in a table is a tr element.

questions.text: Should you use tables to lay out a page?
options: No, tables are for data and CSS handles layout
questions.explanation: Tables are for data. CSS is the right tool for page layout.
